SOME EXPERTS SPECULATE THAT MOORE'S LAW WILL EVENTUALLY HALT DUE TO PHYSICAL LIMITATIONS IN TRANSISTOR SIZE AND HEAT DISSIPATION, LIKELY OCCURRING WITHIN THE NEXT DECADE OR SO.
***
OTHERS ARGUE THAT INNOVATIONS IN THREE-DIMENSIONAL STACKING AND NEW MATERIALS LIKE GRAPHENE COULD EXTEND THE TREND FOR ANOTHER TWENTY YEARS BEFORE A FUNDAMENTAL SHIFT IN COMPUTING ARCHITECTURE BECOMES NECESSARY.
